macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Rally Led by Tech Giants, Nasdaq Hits Record High
items.summary: US indices closed higher with Dow up 0.6% to 49,482, S&P 500 up 0.8% to 6,946, and Nasdaq up 1.3% to 23,152, driven by Nvidia's 1.4% gain ahead of strong earnings beating expectations and projecting $78B revenue. Tech rally extended globally with Nikkei up 2.2% to record 58,583 and European Stoxx 600 up 0.7%.

items.title: Nvidia Delivers Blowout Earnings, Boosts AI Optimism
items.summary: Nvidia reported quarterly profit exceeding expectations and forecasted $78B revenue for the current quarter, surpassing analyst estimates of $72.3B, powering a 1.4% stock rise and broader tech sector gains amid easing AI disruption fears. Salesforce also gained 3% ahead of its earnings.

items.title: Bank of Japan Faces Dovish Pressure from Minister Takaichi
items.summary: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi expressed reservations to BOJ about potential rate hikes, weakening the yen; separately, Minister Se Tichi voiced concerns to Governor Ueda, contributing to JPY depreciation amid global market moves. Geopolitics, including eased Middle East tensions, influenced oil and safe-haven assets.

items.title: Australian CPI Expected to Show Sticky Inflation Ahead
items.summary: Markets anticipate Australian monthly CPI at 0.2% and yearly at 3.7%, with AUD rallying on upbeat data expectations; US Treasury yields rose modestly, 10Y to 4.05%, 2Y to 3.47%.

items.title: HSBC and European Banks Lift Stoxx 600 on Strong Earnings
items.summary: European Stoxx 600 rose 0.7% boosted by bank earnings, with HSBC shares up 6.7% on better-than-expected 2025 results; FTSE 100 up 1.18% to 10,806 amid global tech momentum.

items.title: Geopolitics Overshadows Data, Trump Tariffs Add Pressure
items.summary: Geopolitical updates dominated markets over fundamentals, with eased Iran-US tensions impacting oil; investors also eyed new Trump tariffs replacing Supreme Court-struck ones amid AI winner-loser shifts.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Up to 9% Intraday Amid Market Rebound
items.summary: Crypto assets rebounded sharply with Bitcoin testing higher levels after a marginal recovery in risk appetite, though it later dropped below 68,000 USDT to 67,988 USDT. Altcoins like ETH, DOT, UNI, and NEAR also strengthened significantly.

items.title: NEAR Launches Confidential Intents Privacy Layer, Price Rises Over 17%
items.summary: NEAR Protocol introduced 'Confidential Intents,' a privacy execution layer for cross-chain transactions, driving its token price up more than 17%. This marks a key development in blockchain privacy features.

items.title: Circle Stock Surges 35% on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Circle reported better-than-expected earnings, leading to a 35% surge in its stock price. The results highlight growing adoption of stablecoins like USDC amid broader market recovery.

items.title: U.S. Senator Investigates Binance Over Alleged $1.7B Iran Transfers
items.summary: A U.S. senator has launched an investigation into Binance for allegedly transferring $1.7 billion to Iranian entities, intensifying regulatory scrutiny on the exchange.

items.title: Hong Kong Advances Digital Bond Platform and Tokenization Hub
items.summary: Hong Kong plans to build a digital bond platform linked to a regional tokenization hub, integrating bond settlement into core infrastructure to boost blockchain adoption.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Outlines 7 Hard Forks by 2029
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation announced plans for 7 hard forks by 2029, targeting 5 major milestones including Teragas L2 via data availability sampling and post-quantum cryptography.

items.title: Four Companies Enter UK Stablecoin Regulatory Sandbox
items.summary: Four companies have joined the UK's stablecoin regulatory sandbox, advancing compliant issuance and use of stablecoins in the financial system.

items.title: Kraken Launches Crypto-Backed Staking Loan Product Flexline
items.summary: Kraken introduced 'Flexline,' a new product allowing users to take staking loans backed by crypto assets, expanding DeFi-like services on the platform.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: MIT Researchers Develop TLT Method to Boost LLM Training Efficiency by Up to 210%
items.summary: MIT researchers introduced TLT, a technique using speculative decoding during computational downtime to accelerate reasoning LLM training by 70-210% without accuracy loss, producing a deployable smaller drafter model as a byproduct.

items.title: Nvidia Reports Record $68.1 Billion Q4 Revenue Amid AI Boom
items.summary: Nvidia announced Q4 FY2026 revenue of $68.1 billion, up 73% year-over-year, driven by AI demand; executives predict $3-4 trillion annual AI infrastructure spend by 2029-2030 and shift to agentic AI.

items.title: LLMs Enable Automated Deanonymization of Anonymous Online Posts with 67% Success Rate
items.summary: Researchers demonstrated LLM agents can identify users from anonymized posts on Hacker News, Reddit, and LinkedIn with high precision, scaling to tens of thousands of candidates and raising privacy concerns.

items.title: Mint Showcases First FLOKI Minibot M1 AI Companion Robot Prototype
items.summary: Mint Incorporation delivered the prototype of FLOKI Minibot M1, an AI-powered companion robot developed with Rice Robotics, targeting B2C market with plans for 800 units by Q1 2026 end.

items.title: AI Skills Top Talent Shortage List in Asia Pacific and Middle East
items.summary: ManpowerGroup's 2026 survey finds AI model development and AI literacy as the hardest-to-find skills in APME, outranking traditional IT skills amid accelerating AI adoption.

items.title: Multi-University Project Awarded $800K to Build AI Models Representing Historical Periods
items.summary: Researchers from four universities received Schmidt Sciences funding for 'Artificial Intelligence for Cultural and Historical Reasoning' to train diverse language models mimicking past historical viewpoints.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Marathon Server Slam Open Preview Begins Today
items.summary: The Marathon Server Slam preview opens worldwide on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam from February 26 at 10 a.m. PT to March 2, offering limited maps and rewards for participants and viewers. Full launch follows on March 5 with cross-play support.[1][3]

items.title: Tokyo Xtreme Racer Releases on PlayStation 5
items.summary: Tokyo Xtreme Racer arrives on PS5 today, February 26, featuring new cars, drivable traffic vehicles, and a returning replay mode.[2]

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem Earns High Praise in Review
items.summary: Capcom's Resident Evil Requiem is hailed as one of the best in the series and top games of 2026, with stunning RE Engine visuals, engaging story, and smooth 4K/60 FPS performance on PS5 Pro. Available now on multiple platforms.[5]

items.title: PlayStation Plus Announces March 2026 Monthly Games
items.summary: PlayStation Plus reveals its March lineup including Monster Hunter Rise and others, available March 3 to April 6; February games like Undisputed depart March 2.[4]

items.title: Black Desert Launches Spring Festival Event
items.summary: Black Desert kicks off its Spring Festival with a 1,000% Hot Time event today, February 26, running until March 12 after maintenance.[8]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
